data.title: Equinix opens first AI-ready data center in India's Chennai
data.description:
• Equinix (NASDAQ:EQIX) on Friday announced the opening of its first International Business Exchange data center in Chennai, India—CN1.
  • Located in Siruseri, Tamil Nadu, on a nearly six-acre plot of land, this new facility will be interconnected with Equinix's Mumbai campus, which consists of three IBX data centers.
  • The new facility will support local and global businesses by providing direct access to one of the world's fastest-growing digital economies.

data.title: Minneapolis Fed's Kashkari increased his projection for rate cuts this year due to labor market risks
data.description:
Due to increased risk to the employment side of the Federal Reserve's mandate, Minneapolis Fed President Neel Kashkari penciled in more rate cuts for this year, to three from two. However, he also "nudged up" his long-run federal funds rate outlook by 25 basis points to 3.1%, he wrote in an essay on Friday.

Kashkari sees a rapid weakening of the labor market as a more likely risk than a large upside inflation surprise. That's the reason that he voted for a 25-basis-point rate cut at the Federal Open Market Committee on Wednesday.

While there's still much uncertainty over the ultimate effects of tariffs on prices and how long it will take for the effects to play out, he doesn't expect inflation to spike. "Unless there is some large increase in tariff rates from here or some other supply side shock, it is hard for me to see inflation climbing much higher than 3 percent given announced tariff rates and the relatively small share of imported goods in overall U.S. consumption," he wrote.

Meanwhile, past economy cycles demonstrate that "when labor markets weaken, they can weaken quickly and non-linearly."

The essay also discussed the mismatch between the labor market and the financial markets, and considered three potential explanations. One is that any sign of new economic weakness could burst the risk markets' exuberance, bringing the two into alignment. Another is that markets expect the FOMC to embark on a series of rate cuts as they'll decide that tariff inflation will be transitory. The third is that the neutral rate of interest — the point at which interest rates neither stimulate nor suppress the economy — has increased, at least in the near-term.

"Over the past few years, I have continued to increase my assessment of the neutral rate of interest," he wrote. "The implication of this reassessment is that monetary policy has likely not been as tight as I previously understood."

Still, he emphasizes that the Fed shouldn't set a course for a series of rate cuts. "If the labor market proves more resilient than it seems at the moment or if inflation surprises to the upside, we should be prepared to pause and hold our policy rate," he explained. "I even remain open to raising the policy rate further if economic conditions warrant it." 

However, if the labor markets weaken more quickly than the Fed policymakers expect, they could always adjust its rate more quickly to support the economy, he concluded.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: AI winner: Wayfair sees a surge of traffic from LLMs such as ChatGPT and Perplexity
data.description:
Jefferies highlighted on Friday that Wayfair (NYSE:W) is leading its coverage universe in terms of monetizing LLM (Large Language Model) traffic.

After digging into the data, analyst Jonathan Matuszewski and his team determined that 20% of referral visits to Wayfair.com stem from ChatGPT and Perplexity. In August 2025, LLMs like ChatGPT and Perplexity generated more than 500,000 visits to Wayfair.com in the United States. Notably, in August 2024, that ratio of LLM traffic was just 1%. "Wayfair's co-founder & CEO, Niraj Shah, has confirmed collaboration with OpenAI and Perplexity, and we see opportunity ahead as these efforts optimize both conversion and advertising spend," wrote Matuszewski.

Matuszewski also outlined that broad analysis from Adobe shows consumers referred by LLMs visit more pages per session and have lower bounce rates on e-commerce sites relative to consumers visiting via non-A.I. referrals. In addition, conversion rates from LLM referrals are improving.

Jefferies has a Buy rating on Wayfair (NYSE:W) and a price target of $91.

Shares of Wayfair (W) were up 0.6% in premarket trading to $88.90 vs. the 52-week range of $20.41 to $91.77. The e-commerce stock is up 11% over the last six weeks.

The impact of LLMs on referral traffic is expected to be a major factor in which e-commerce sites outperform during the upcoming holiday season.

data.title: Alvotech gets marketing approval in Japan for three new biosimilars
data.description:
• Alvotech (NASDAQ:ALVO) Friday said that its commercialization partner in Japan, Fuji Pharma Co., Ltd., has received marketing approval for three new biosimilars from the Japanese Ministry of Health, Labor, and Welfare. 
  • The biosimilars approved for the Japanese market are AVT03, a biosimilar to Ranmark (denosumab), AVT05, a biosimilar to Simponi (golimumab) and AVT06, a biosimilar to Eylea (aflibercept).
  • Based on publicly available information, AVT05 is the first golimumab biosimilar to be approved for sale in major markets globally.
  • Alvotech (NASDAQ:ALVO) +1% premarket.

data.title: M/I Homes amends, extends credit facility
data.description:
M/I Homes (MHO) announced on Friday an amendment to its credit agreement, which increases borrowing availability from $650M to $900M and extends the maturity to September 2030.

"Our financial condition is very strong with zero borrowings under our existing credit facility, a cash position of $800 million, homebuilding debt-to-capital of 18%, and a net debt-to-capital ratio of negative 3% at June 30, 2025," said CEO Robert Schottenstein.

"Extending our credit facility to a five-year term and the $250 million of increased commitment amounts from our lenders provides additional liquidity and financial flexibility that further strengthens our company as we look to the future," said Schottenstein.

The company said there were no borrowings outstanding at the closing date.

data.title: Chijet Motor appoints blockchain veteran COO to spearhead crypto treasury overhaul
data.description:
Chijet Motor Company (NASDAQ:CJET) stock was surging after the fuel vehicles company announced the appointment of blockchain veteran Jason Miller as COO to spearhead its crypto treasury overhaul.

Shares were +111.75% Friday pre-market to $0.21.

The company said this move positions it to integrate major cryptocurrencies into its balance sheet.

"The institutional adoption wave is here, and companies that move decisively into digital asset treasury management are being rewarded with extraordinary shareholder returns," said Miller.

data.title: Merck wins EU backing for Keytruda injectable
data.description:
Merck (NYSE:MRK) announced on Friday that an expert panel of the EU drug regulator, the European Medicines Agency (EMA) endorsed an injectable version of its blockbuster cancer therapy Keytruda (pembrolizumab).

The New Jersey-based pharma giant said that EMA’s Committee for Medicinal Products for Human Use (CHMP) has issued a positive opinion recommending the product, which, if approved by the European Commission, could be marketed as Keytruda SC.

The CHMP’s decision, backed by data from a pivotal trial called 3475A-D77, will next undergo EC's review after which a final determination on the company’s marketing authorization is expected in Q4 2025.

If approved, Keytruda SC will be available in EU countries as well as Iceland, Liechtenstein, and Norway for all adult indications approved for the anti-PD-1 therapy, Merck (NYSE:MRK) said.

The injectable, designed to be administered once every three weeks or once in every six weeks by a healthcare provider across multiple settings, is expected to increase the drug’s availability ahead of a loss of patent exclusivity for the intravenous version in 2028.

Keytruda SC combines pembrolizumab, the active ingredient of Keytruda, with a version of human hyaluronidase developed and manufactured by Korean biotech Alteogen.

data.title: Apple used AI for high blood pressure detection feature in new smartwatch: report
data.description:
• Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL) used artificial intelligence, rather than traditional blood pressure monitoring, for the new high blood pressure detection feature in the Apple Watch Series 11, Reuters reported. 
  • The tech giant used AI models and applied them to existing sensor data, Apple health executive Sumbul Ahmad Desai told the news outlet. AI was then utilized to sift through data from 100,000 people who enrolled in a heart study that was launched in 2019. This was done to see whether the signal data from the heart-related sensor in the Apple Watch could align with traditional blood pressure measurements, Desai added.
  • From there, the company created an algorithm that validated the findings in a separate study of 2,000 people. Apple recently received approval from the U.S. Food and Drug Administration for the new feature.
  • The revelation comes as Apple has been criticized by many experts and onlookers for falling behind the race for artificial intelligence supremacy, ceding the race to other tech companies. 
  • All the new products announced at Apple's fall event earlier this month — including the Apple Watch Series 11 and new iPhone Air — are available for purchase today. 
  • Apple shares rose 0.7% in premarket trading on Friday.
